pattern: Each prime factor is distributed independently into the array's slots, so the count is the product over primes of stars-and-bars combinations, taken modulo 1e9+7.
hints: Factorize k into primes; placing a prime's exponent among n slots is independent of the other primes.
Distributing e identical copies of a prime into n positions is a stars-and-bars count: C(e+n-1, n-1).
Multiply C(e+n-1, n-1) over all prime exponents e of k, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: first sample with a large modular result
second sample including primes and composites
single slot must equal k exactly, one way
product one in many slots, one way

stress.description: many random queries across the full bounds

complexity.time: O(maxv log log maxv + Q log k)
